articles.title: Read the Supreme Court Decision in the Birthright Citizenship Case
articles.abstract: In the citizenship case, the justices voted 6-3, with the liberals in dissent, to limit the lower-courts’ preliminary injunctions to each plaintiff with standing to sue. The majority stressed that it is not addressing the merits of Trump’s attempt to end automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil to undocumented migrants and foreign visitors without green cards.
articles.snippet: In the citizenship case, the justices voted 6-3, with the liberals in dissent, to limit the lower-courts’ preliminary injunctions to each plaintiff with standing to sue. The majority stressed that it is not addressing the merits of Trump’s attempt to end automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil to undocumented migrants and foreign visitors without green cards.

articles.title: Read the document
articles.abstract: In a 220-page report to FEMA last year, Kerr County said a flood was likely within a year, and could exceed historical records.

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court’s Decision on Trump’s Sweeping Tariffs
articles.abstract: In a major setback for President Trump’s economic agenda, the court ruled that he could not invoke an emergency power to set tariffs on imports.
articles.snippet: In a major setback for President Trump’s economic agenda, the court ruled that he could not invoke an emergency power to set tariffs on imports.

articles.title: Can Nations Agree How to Mine the Sea? This Is the Year, She Says.
articles.abstract: Leticia Carvalho heads a global authority that’s been struggling to set rules for a decade. President Trump’s aggressive push on ocean mining makes her task more urgent.
articles.snippet: Leticia Carvalho heads a global authority that’s been struggling to set rules for a decade. President Trump’s aggressive push on ocean mining makes her task more urgent.

articles.title: MAHA Moms Turn Against Trump: ‘Women Feel Like They Were Lied To’
articles.abstract: President Trump’s executive order aimed at spurring production of a pesticide has infuriated leaders of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s MAHA movement.
articles.snippet: President Trump’s executive order aimed at spurring production of a pesticide has infuriated leaders of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s MAHA movement.
articles.byline: By Sheryl Gay Stolberg and Hiroko Tabuchi

articles.title: I Saw a Child Who Seemed Neglected. Should I Have Done Something?
articles.abstract: I worry that I’m piling assumptions onto a family I know nothing about, a family that might be doing its best.

articles.title: Read the Final Report on the Lewiston, Maine, Mass Shootings
articles.abstract: An independent commission investigating the October 2023 shooting emphasized failures by both the local police and the Army Reserve.
